# pycubrid

> Pure Python DB-API 2.0 (PEP 249) driver for the CUBRID relational database. No C extensions, no compilation ŌĆö install with `pip install pycubrid` and connect immediately. Python 3.10+.

pycubrid implements the CUBRID CAS (Client Application Server) wire protocol directly in Python, providing a zero-dependency database driver that works everywhere Python runs. It is the recommended driver for sqlalchemy-cubrid.

Key facts:
- PEP 249 compliant: `apilevel = "2.0"`, `threadsafety = 1`, `paramstyle = "qmark"`
- Connect: `pycubrid.connect(host="localhost", port=33000, database="testdb", user="dba")`
- Full exception hierarchy: `Warning`, `Error`, `InterfaceError`, `DatabaseError`, `OperationalError`, `IntegrityError`, `InternalError`, `ProgrammingError`, `NotSupportedError`
- Standard type objects: `STRING`, `BINARY`, `NUMBER`, `DATETIME`, `ROWID`
- Features: prepared statements, batch operations (`executemany`), LOB support (CLOB/BLOB), schema introspection, context managers, iterator protocol
- PEP 561 typed package with `py.typed` marker
- 471 offline tests, 99%+ code coverage
- Install: `pip install pycubrid`

## Docs

- [Connection Guide](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/docs/CONNECTION.md): Connection strings, URL format, configuration, connection pooling
- [Type Mapping](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/docs/TYPES.md): Full type mapping between Python and CUBRID types
- [API Reference](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/docs/API_REFERENCE.md): Complete API documentation ŌĆö modules, classes, functions
- [Protocol Reference](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/docs/PROTOCOL.md): CAS wire protocol specification
- [Examples](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/docs/EXAMPLES.md): Practical usage examples with code
- [Development Guide](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md): Dev setup, testing, Docker, coverage, CI/CD

## Examples

- [Quick Start](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/README.md#quick-start): Basic connection, context manager, parameter binding, prepared statements
- [CUBRID Cookbook ŌĆö pycubrid](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-cookbook/tree/main/python/pycubrid): 6 production-ready examples ŌĆö connect, CRUD, transactions, prepared statements, error handling, LOBs

## API Reference

- [Source ŌĆö __init__.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/pycubrid/__init__.py): Public API ŌĆö connect(), types, exceptions, __version__
- [Source ŌĆö connection.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/pycubrid/connection.py): Connection class ŌĆö connect, commit, rollback, cursor, LOB
- [Source ŌĆö cursor.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/pycubrid/cursor.py): Cursor class ŌĆö execute, fetch, prepare, callproc, iterator
- [Source ŌĆö types.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/pycubrid/types.py): DB-API 2.0 type objects and constructors
- [Source ŌĆö exceptions.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/pycubrid/exceptions.py): PEP 249 exception hierarchy

## Ecosystem

- [sqlalchemy-cubrid](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid): SQLAlchemy 2.0 dialect for CUBRID (uses pycubrid as driver)
- [cubrid-cookbook](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-cookbook): Production-ready examples across Python, Node.js, and Go
- [CUBRID](https://www.cubrid.org/): The CUBRID database

## Optional

- [Product Requirements](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/docs/PRD.md): Product requirements document with Example-first Design Philosophy
- [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines
